series_description: (a) Cash Expenditure Vouchers (ADM 3151.1 and 3151.2) with attached documents such as invoices or statements of amount due for goods and services rendered; subsidy payments to institutions, debt service, or transfers and non-expense disbursements; relative copy of encumbrance; correspondence if necessary for a complete understanding of the payment being made; travel expense report (ADM 3148); and copy of emergency board request (EB-1). (b) Intra-State Transfer Voucher (ADM-3101) with attached documents such as invoices of amount due for goods or services received from another State agency and copy of encumbrance.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 3 years, in records storage 3 years or 6 months after the State and Federal audits whichever is longer ; Electronic: Retain 6 years or 6 months after the State and Federal audits whichever is longer

series_description: Documents listed in (a) through (c.) are prepared yearly for the State Office of Budget & Management Data shown on these documents is also used by Department of Administrative Service State Accounting Group at the beginning of the fiscal year to set up this agency''s fiscal year appropriations by budget quarter on the transaction register (ADM 3107). Our accounting office uses them to set up our ledger accounts. (a.) Fiscal Year Allotment Plan Worksheet OBM 266. (b.) Fiscal Year Allotment Plan Worksheet Special Purpose and Subsidies OBM 266 A. (c.) Revenue Estimates - Rotary OBM 266 G (d.) Allotment Requests (ADM-3147) are sent to Office of Budget & Management periodically when there is a need to transfer funds from one budget quarter to another or form one fiscal year to another. (e.) Miscellaneous Internal Budget Control Data is usually in memoranda, worksheet, or resolution form.
retention_statement: Paper: 3 years provided the state audit is complete and discrepancies resolved ; Electronic: 3 years provided the state audit is complete and discrepancies resolved.

series_description: a.)Executed contracts with consultants for personal services rendered, b.) reciprocity agreements, c) contracts for temporary office help, d.) Inter-agency agreements such as rental of data processing services, and i.) other legally binding contracts that are executed by the agency
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 16 years following termination of the contract, or until audit is conducted and all audit discrepancies are resolved, whichever is longer ; Electronic: Retain 16 years following termination of the contract, or until audit is conducted and all audit discrepancies are resolved, whichever is longer.

series_description: Applications and correspondence from students related to this program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain applications and correspondence from awardees and Attorney General''s Office in office for 5 years after final payment made by student. After audit by Auditor of State is completed and released and Federal Government audit, if any, destroy. ; Electronic: Retain data entry SSN and payment history from Attorney General''s Office (including interest paid) 5 years after final payment made by student. After audit by Auditor of State is completed and released and Federal Government audit, if any, destroy.

series_description: Payment requests and correspondence from parents, students and college financial aid officers related to this program.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain correspondence from institutions and students and copies of checks received from institutions in office 5 years. After audit by Auditor of State is completed and released, destroy. ; Electronic: Retain payment and refund information entered on-line through the HEI system ,for 5 years. After audit by Auditor of State is completed and released, destroy.

series_description: These web pages are not maintained by the Chancellor of the Ohio Board of Regents (the “Chancellor”), but are maintained by a social media web page, for example, Facebook or Twitter. The Chancellor may regularly post information on these social media web pages. Records in this series may include, but are not limited to, general descriptive information about the Chancellor, press releases and articles that are of interest to the Chancellor. Information posted that falls under a separate retention schedule will be kept according to that retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain on web page for six months.

series_description: Included are grant applications, budget revisions and related materials generated or maintained by the Adult Basic Literacy Education (ABLE) staff and pertaining to the ABLE grant program.
retention_statement: Paper: Maintain 3 years from the final expenditure report of each approved fiscal year’s grant project, or as required by federal law whichever is longer. ; Electronic: Maintain 3 years from the final expenditure report of each approved fiscal year’s grant project, or as required by federal law whichever is longer.

series_description: Background pieces provide summaries of requests by public institutions requesting approval and independent and out-of-state institutions requesting Certificates of Authorization for new degrees/degree programs.
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent ; Electronic: Permanent

series_description: All drafts of agency documents including, but not limited to, publications, minutes, reports
retention_statement: Electronic: Until superseded by another draft or the final version of the document ; Paper: Until superseded by another draft or the final version of the document

series_description: These web pages contain information made available to the public via the Internet as a matter of convenience. Records in this series may include, but are not limited to, general descriptive information about the Chancellor and the Board of Regents, frequently asked questions, practice and procedure, bulletins and sample forms, announcements, directives, annual reports, and meeting schedules. Hard copies of this information are maintained by the Chancellor under separate retention schedules.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain online until content information is obsolete, replaced, or superseded.

series_description: This record series includes requests for proposals, proposals, proposal evaluation materials, annual reports, correspondence to participating institutions and other related documents
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records may be scanned into the computer and ; Electronic: Retain 3 years following the end of the award cycle

series_description: Includes prined photos as well as negatives and electronic files and related materials
retention_statement: Paper: Until no longer of administrative value and then, if the records appear to have historical value to State . If no historical value is perceived by agency staff, destroy ; Electronic: Until no longer of administrative value and then, if the records appear to have historical value to State Archives. If no historical value is perceived by agency staff, destroy.

series_description: Includes exhibits, movies, and videotape productions.
retention_statement: Electronic: Until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Course or program equivalency approvals related to the Ohio Transfer Module (OTM), Transfer Assurance Guides (TAGs), Career-Technical Assurance Guides (CTAGs), Statewide Apprenticeship Pathways, and any other state-sponsored course or program equivalency initiatives. Documents include: course syllabi, institutional accreditation documents, instructor accreditation documents, and pacing documents (for Career-Technical programs).
retention_statement: Electronic: Permanent

series_description: Meeting minutes, summative reports, and policy documents related to any legislation surrounding Articulation and Transfer (in particular, HB95 (
retention_statement: Paper: Permanent retention of this documents. Paper records may be scanned and destroyed. ; Electronic: Permanent retention.

series_description: Records pertaining to the Research Incentive Program including, but not limited to: Requests for Proposals; proposals submitted by each campus; External reviewer reports from peer review panel members; A final report summarizing the review findings and funding recommendations going to the Chancellor; Reports from the campuses on their use of funds, including such reports as expenditures, success stories, and leverage of external funds; and annual reports on research and development activities at each campus.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years following the end of each awarded program''s funding

series_description: Proclamations are periodically issued by the Chancellor to honor members of the Board of Regents, agency staff, or other individuals, groups and events at the Chancellor''''''''s discretion. They are ceremonial in nature and do not reflect official business of the Chancellor.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years ; Electronic: Retain 2 years

series_description: The Privacy Impact Assessment is a document that describes the personal information collected by an information system, the impact to operations if a breach or loss of availability were to occur, the legal authority by which the department collects the information, and the security controls required to protect the data. This document is mandated by multiple sections of Ohio Revised Code and by state IT policy.
retention_statement: Paper: Until superseded or system is no longer active ; Electronic: Until superseded or system is no longer active

series_title: John R. Justice Student Loan Repayment Program
series_description: Request checks from OBM, applications, awardee and non-awardee data and correspondence from applicants related to this program. Other related records
retention_statement: Paper: Retain awardee records until cancellation/forgiveness or payment in full ; Electronic: Retain awardee records until cancellation/forgiveness or payment in full

series_description: All records concerning federal grants to OBR which document the expenditure and use of federal monies. Also, requests for proposals, proposals, evaluation materials, reports and other related materials. This schedule only covers federal grant programs that do not have an individual record retention schedule.
retention_statement: Electronic: 3 years from the date of the submission of the final report to the awarding federal agency or as otherwise agreed between OBR and the awarding agency, whichever is longer.In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved. ; Paper: Paper records may be scanned and destroyed. 3 years from the date of the submission of the final report to the awarding federal agency or as otherwise agreed between OBR and the awarding agency, whichever is longer.In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved.

series_description: Quarterly financial reports (unaudited) from state colleges and universities. Schedule QF: Quarterly Report of Financial Actions; Schedule QF-2: Statement of Current Funds Revenues, Expenditures, and Other Changes Year to Date; Certification Form; and Quarterly letters
retention_statement: Paper: May be scanned to the L: drive and ; Electronic: Retain 3 years

series_description: Records pertaining to the Ohio Education Technology Conference including, but not limited to, reports, requests for quotations, requests for proposals, promotional materials, and forms. Contracts and agreements related to the conference are maintained according to the Board of Regents retention schedule for all such records.
retention_statement: Paper: Scan into the computer and destroy. If paper records are not scanned, retain 3 years following the end of the annual conference ; Electronic: Retain 3 years following the end of the annual conference

series_description: All records concerning private grants to OBR which document the expenditure and use of private dollars. Also, requests for proposals, proposals, evaluation materials, reports and other related materials. This schedule only covers private grant programs that do not have an individual record retention schedule.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records may be scanned and destroyed. Retain 3 years from the date of the submission of the final report or as otherwise agreed between OBR and the party awarding the grant, whichever is longer. In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved. ; Electronic: Retain 3 years from the date of the submission of the final report or as otherwise agreed between OBR and the party awarding the grant, whichever is longer. In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved.

series_description: Includes records for the following grant programs which were both state and federally funded: Green Pathways grant program, H-1B Technical Skills training grant, and the Ohio Skills Bank grant program. These records include reports and financial records.
retention_statement: Paper: Paper records may be scanned and destroyed. Paper records that are not scanned should be retained for three years following the end of the grant period.In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved. ; Electronic: Retain for three years following the end of the grant period.In the event of an open audit on these records at the end of the retention period, retain until the audit is complete and discrepancies resolved.

series_description: This form is required to be signed by independent contractors as a condition of receiving a contract with the Ohio Board of Regents.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 5 years from receipt Paper records may be immediately scanned and deleted. ; Electronic: Retain 5 years from receipt

series_description: Coding correction entries and budget transfers
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until audited by Auditor of State and audit report is released and all discrepancies are resolved

series_description: Vouchers related to bond-funded capital projects
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 23 years ; Electronic: Retain 23 years

series_title: Confidential Personal Information (CPI) Systems Access Logs
series_description: Manual and electronic logs documenting employee access to confidential personal information (CPI) pursuant to
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 2 years ; Electronic: Retain 2 years

series_description: Newsletters released by staff at the Ohio Board of Regents.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 2 years and

series_description: Reports created by OBR that are neither mandated by law nor requested by an outside person or entity.
retention_statement: Paper: If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists ; Electronic: If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists

series_title: Workforce Development Equipment and Facility Grant
series_description: Includes records related to the Workforce Development Equipment and Facility Grant. These records include, but are not limited to: Requests for Proposals, proposals, reports, and award letters.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain 3 years following the end of the grant cycle ; Electronic: Retain 3 years following the end of the grant cycle

series_description: The Ohio Board of Regents uses a Sharepoint Tracking System to track and monitor the status of contracts, MOU''s, agreements and other legally binding documents. Any records that are stored on the tracking system (completed contracts, drafts, etc...) are also stored elsewhere and covered by individual retention schedules. This schedule covers the information provided by the requester in the tracking system as well as emails sent out by the system.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain until no longer of administrative value

series_description: Includes letters to and from attorneys discussing official agency business, opinion letters, and other letters signed by OBR attorneys. Letters related to contracts and agreements will be kept as long as the retention schedule for the contract or agreement. Further, this record series only includes letters that are not related to records with other retention schedules.
retention_statement: Paper: 5 years from creation or receipt of letter ; Electronic: 5 years from creation or receipt of letter

series_description: All reports mandated by law to be created by the Ohio Board of Regents. This record series includes but is not limited to the annual report on the condition of higher education (see Ohio Revised Code 3333.032) as well as the reports required pursuant to Ohio Revised Code 3333.041.
retention_statement: Electronic: Deliver an electronic version to the State Library. If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists ; Paper: Deliver an electronic version to the State Library. If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists

series_description: Records used by the Chancellor and agency staff to make a determination regarding the issuance of directives. Includes but is not limited to documentation provided by institutions and third parties, staff research on the topic and public comments.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years ; Electronic: 10 years

series_description: Directives issued by the Chancellor of the Ohio Board of Regents for the purpose of carrying out his or her duties and responsibilities.
retention_statement: Electronic: Permanent

series_description: Records used by the Chancellor and agency staff to make determinations regarding the issuance of directives. Includes, but is not limited to, information provided by institutions and other third parties, staff research and public comments.
retention_statement: Electronic: 10 years ; Paper: 10 years

series_description: Reports requested by colleges and universities, the governor’s office, state agencies and other officials or entities.
retention_statement: Paper: If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists ; Electronic: If OBR perceives that the report has lasting significance to the research/policy making capabilities of OBR, maintain until such significance no longer exists

series_description: This record series includes requests for proposals, proposals, evaluation materials, annual reports, correspondence and other documents related to the Changing Campus Culture program.
retention_statement: Paper: paper records may be scanned and destroyed. Otherwise, retain for four years following the end of the award period and ; Electronic: Retain four years following the end of the award period and

series_description: This record series includes complaints received by the Ohio Department of Higher Education related to Ohio public and private institutions of higher education. It includes any attachments or documents related to the complaint, all agency responses to such complaint and communications with any institution regarding the complaint.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain 5 years from ODHE''s final response to the complaint

series_description: Records from Governor’s office initiatives that the agency is asked to participate in
retention_statement: Paper: Retain in office 5 years; Record Center 10 years or until state/federal audit has been performed and audit report released

series_description: Inactive Action Fund Proposals(Capital funded proposals projects required university match and Regents match)
retention_statement: Paper: Retain for 5 years
